description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/salvus/salvus:latest for all jobs and a global before_script that ensures ssh-agent is installed, starts the agent, adds the $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, configures SSH to skip host key checking, and prepends /miniconda/bin to PATH. Define three stages: upstream, install-and-test, and deploy. In the upstream stage add an upstream-dependencies job that runs source activate salvus, creates the UpstreamDependencies directory, clones pyexodus and the salvus_wave repository (using CI_JOB_TOKEN), and stores the UpstreamDependencies directory as an artifact. In the install-and-test stage add a salvus-seismo job that depends on upstream-dependencies, activates the salvus environment, installs the pyexodus package in editable mode, installs the salvus_wave Python package with CFLAGS=-I/usr/include/eigen3, installs flake8 and toml via pip, installs the local py package, and finally runs py.test. In the deploy stage add an update_meta_repo job that runs only on the master branch (excluding triggers), uses the shared runner tag, and executes a python script ./conf/run_dependencies.py with the arguments 3238505, ${SALVUS_META_TOKEN}, $PRIV_TOKEN, $SRC_BRANCH, and $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME.
